primary_hypothesis: A realistic regional network can identify high-risk Manila Trench events earlier than conventional reliable characterization while meeting a continuous-stream false-alarm constraint under held-out rupture and noise domains.

decision_rule.success: Held-out data meet both operational detection and reliable-magnitude gates, and characterization gain is reported against every available conventional comparator with scenario-specific tsunami arrivals.
decision_rule.conditional_GNN: Train a graph model only after a coherent physical baseline demonstrates held-out signal; accept it only if it improves the same frozen metrics and splits.
decision_rule.negative_result: Failure below a magnitude, station count, noise level, segment, or outage condition is reported as a network feasibility boundary.
decision_rule.failure: A claim based on arrival precedence alone, conditional-on-event accuracy without continuous false alarms, or synthetic white noise alone.
